description: Web application attack-path realizability: recon through initial access.

WB-01.label: http_service_reachable
WB-01.description: Target exposes an HTTP/HTTPS service on at least one port.
WB-01.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): TCP connect + HTTP response received.

WB-02.label: server_version_disclosed
WB-02.description: HTTP response headers or body disclose server software and version.
WB-02.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Server, X-Powered-By, or Via header contains version string.

WB-03.label: verbose_error_pages
WB-03.description: Application returns stack traces, SQL errors, or debug output on malformed input.
WB-03.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Response to malformed request contains traceback, SQL syntax error, or framework debug page.

WB-04.label: directory_listing_enabled
WB-04.description: At least one directory returns an auto-generated index listing.
WB-04.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Response body matches directory listing pattern (Index of /, Parent Directory).

WB-05.label: sensitive_paths_exposed
WB-05.description: Sensitive files or paths are accessible without authentication (.git, .env, backup files, admin panels).
WB-05.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): HTTP 200 on /.git/HEAD, /.env, /backup, /admin, /phpmyadmin, or similar.

WB-06.label: login_form_present
WB-06.description: Application exposes a login form or authentication endpoint.
WB-06.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Response contains <form> with password-type input or known auth endpoint returns 200/401.

WB-07.label: no_rate_limiting
WB-07.description: Login or input endpoints accept rapid repeated requests without throttling or lockout.
WB-07.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): 10+ sequential requests to auth endpoint return consistent timing and status codes, no CAPTCHA or 429.

WB-08.label: default_credentials_accepted
WB-08.description: Application accepts known default username/password combinations.
WB-08.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Login with default creds (admin/admin, admin/password, etc.) returns authenticated response.

WB-09.label: injectable_parameter_found
WB-09.description: At least one input parameter exhibits behavior consistent with SQL injection (error-based, blind, or time-based).
WB-09.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Single-quote payload triggers SQL error, boolean condition changes response, or time-delay payload causes measurable delay.

WB-10.label: sqli_data_extraction_confirmed
WB-10.description: SQL injection yields actual data extraction (table names, column names, row data).
WB-10.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): UNION-based or error-based extraction returns database content in response.

WB-11.label: reflected_xss_present
WB-11.description: User input is reflected in the response without sanitization, allowing script injection.
WB-11.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Injected payload appears unescaped in response body.

WB-12.label: path_traversal_present
WB-12.description: Application is vulnerable to directory traversal, exposing files outside the web root.
WB-12.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): ../../etc/passwd or equivalent payload returns file contents.

WB-13.label: file_upload_unrestricted
WB-13.description: Application accepts file uploads without adequate type/extension validation.
WB-13.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Upload of .php/.jsp/.aspx file accepted and accessible at predictable path.

WB-14.label: command_injection_present
WB-14.description: User input is passed to a system command without sanitization.
WB-14.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Command separator payload (;, |, &&) causes measurable side effect (time delay, DNS lookup, or output change).

WB-15.label: ssrf_present
WB-15.description: Application can be induced to make requests to attacker-controlled or internal endpoints.
WB-15.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): URL parameter pointing to internal address or callback server triggers request from target.

WB-16.label: session_token_predictable
WB-16.description: Session tokens lack sufficient entropy or follow a predictable pattern.
WB-16.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Multiple session tokens collected show low entropy or sequential pattern.

WB-17.label: tls_misconfigured
WB-17.description: TLS configuration is weak: expired cert, self-signed, weak ciphers, or SSLv3/TLS1.0 supported.
WB-17.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): TLS handshake reveals weak cipher suite, old protocol, or cert validation failure.

WB-18.label: cors_misconfigured
WB-18.description: CORS policy allows arbitrary origins or reflects the Origin header with credentials allowed.
WB-18.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflects attacker origin with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true.

WB-19.label: security_headers_missing
WB-19.description: Response lacks critical security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options).
WB-19.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Response headers do not include one or more of CSP, X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security.

WB-20.label: database_user_privileged
WB-20.description: The database user behind the application has elevated privileges (FILE, DBA, xp_cmdshell, SUPER).
WB-20.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): SQLi-based privilege check reveals FILE_PRIV=Y, DBA role, or ability to execute OS commands.

WB-21.label: webshell_upload_achieved
WB-21.description: A webshell or reverse shell payload has been uploaded and is executable on the target.
WB-21.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Uploaded payload responds to command execution request.

WB-22.label: ssti_present
WB-22.description: Server-side template injection allows execution of template expressions in the response.
WB-22.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): Template expression (e.g. {{7*7}} returning 49) is evaluated and returned in response.

WB-23.label: api_endpoint_unauthenticated
WB-23.description: API endpoints return sensitive data or accept mutations without authentication.
WB-23.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): GET/POST to /api/* paths returns data or accepts changes with no auth header.

WB-24.label: robots_sitemap_disclosure
WB-24.description: robots.txt or sitemap.xml reveals hidden paths, admin endpoints, or internal structure.
WB-24.evidence_hint: Evidence rank 1 (runtime): robots.txt Disallow entries or sitemap URLs point to non-obvious paths.

web_sqli_to_shell_v1.description: SQL injection leading to OS command execution or file write, achieving remote code execution.

web_sqli_data_theft_v1.description: SQL injection leading to extraction of credentials, PII, or application secrets.

web_upload_to_shell_v1.description: Unrestricted file upload leading to webshell execution.

web_cmdi_to_shell_v1.description: Command injection through web input leading to OS command execution.

web_ssti_to_rce_v1.description: Server-side template injection escalated to remote code execution.

web_path_traversal_cred_theft_v1.description: Path traversal used to read sensitive files containing credentials or keys.

web_default_creds_to_admin_v1.description: Default credentials grant administrative access to the application.

web_ssrf_internal_access_v1.description: SSRF used to access internal services, metadata endpoints, or pivot to internal network.

web_source_disclosure_to_foothold_v1.description: Exposed source code, git repositories, or config files reveal credentials or internal architecture enabling further attack.
